text: Nyandarua police have launched investigations into claims by East Africa Legislative Assembly MP Kanini Kega that his vehicle was sprayed with bullets by people he suspects were police officers Read more;
Nimrod Mbae, Secretary for Planning, DCP, asked Murkomen to arrest party leader Gachagua if he has violated the law. "If you think Gachagua has offended any law, go ahead and arrest him,” said Mbae.
"Murkomen, you should know that I was locked up in a police cell while defending Ruto, the same person who gave you your job. I never abandoned him." Rigathi Gachagua.

Deputy President Kithure Kindiki on Thursday acknowledged UDA’s defeat in the Ol Kalou by-election, describing the outcome as leaving the ruling coalition and the opposition tied at 1-1 ahead of the 2027 General Election.

RT @ICJKenya: Ahead of the 2027 General Election, Kenya is witnessing a troubling resurgence of politically sponsored "goons”, a trend that threatens democratic freedoms, civic space, and the constitutional rights of all Kenyans. The use of outsourced violence to disrupt political rallies, intimidate opponents, and silence dissent is a form of informal repression. It undermines political tolerance, erodes public trust, and enables those responsible to evade accountability. Speaking during an interview with @africanews, ICJ Kenya Executive Director @kipdemas, alongside @OduorErnest, Head of Communications at the Kenya Human Rights Commission (@thekhrc), pointed out the urgent need for professional, accountable policing. "We need an independent police force that is governed by the Constitution, is democratic, and works based on intelligence and the needs of the communities." Demas Kiprono. As the country prepares for the 2027 elections, safeguarding human rights, protecting peaceful assembly, and ensuring every Kenyan can participate freely in democratic processes must remain a national priority. #RuleOfLaw #HumanRights #elections2027 #ICJKenya

AN INNOCENT LIFE CUT SHORT. JUSTICE FOR LAURA WESONGA MUST NOT WAIT I am deeply saddened by the tragic death of Ms. Laura Wesonga, a KMTC Busia Campus student, who was fatally shot during a police operation in Marachi Estate, Busia Town yesterday evening. I extend my heartfelt condolences to her family, friends, fellow students, and the people of Busia. The police have a constitutional duty to protect life. No law enforcement operation should result in the loss of innocent lives. Every operation must be conducted with professionalism, restraint, and full respect for the Constitution. I therefore call for an immediate, independent, and transparent investigation by @IPOA_KE with any officer found responsible held fully accountable. The @NPSOfficial_KE operations should be intelligence led, professional policing that protects innocent citizens while firmly dealing with crime. The political temperatures in Busia must cool down immediately. I urge political leaders across Busia and the wider nation to fully embrace democracy. In a democracy, dissent is a right. Not everyone must support you. Using hired goons to intimidate rivals and settle political scores is primitive, illegal, and unacceptable. #JusticeforLauraWesonga must be swift, transparent, and uncompromising. The people of Busia deserve security, accountability, and respect for the sanctity of human life.
